name: Chocolate-Dipped Coconut Shortbread
recipeIngredient: 6 ounces Semisweet chocolate chips; melted with
recipeInstructions:
Recipe by: The Joy of Cookies
Buttery shortbread laced with coconut and dipped into dark
chocolate.
Combine flour, baking powder, salt; set aside. Beat together butter,
sugar and vanilla until light and fluffy. Stir in four 1/2 cup at a time
and mix until well-blended. Stir in coconut. Form dough into a 4-inch x 7-inch
rectangle. Wrap and refrigerate until firm, 2 hours. Preheat oven to 300 degrees.
Butter two large baking sheets. On floured surface, roll dough into an 8-by-14-inch
rectangle about 1/4-inch thick. Using floured knife with long blade, cut into 24,
2-inch squares. Arrange squares, 1-inch apart, on buttered sheets. Bake 25-30 minutes until golden.
Cool on racks. Line two large baking sheets with waxed paper, set aside. Melt chocolate and
butter together, stirring to blend well. Dip half of each cookie diagonally into chocolate,
shaking off excess. Arrange on paper-lined sheets. Refrigerate until chocolate sets, about 10 minutes.
Store airtight at room temperature or freeze longer.
